'''Galasett''' was a [[Kerestian/Legends|Kerestian]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] who tracked outlaw bounty hunters. He started doing so when two overzealous bounty hunters brutally killed his friend [[Reglis Taal]]'s brother. When he discovered it, he decided that he would become a vigilante for other hunters. As Galasett himself was an overkill, he eventually went to jail and paid fines for the collateral damage he caused during the hunts.

Galasett met and fought [[Baraduk]], a Barabel bounty hunter. Both of them were injured in the fight, and Baraduk escaped Galasett. Galasett developed a personal hatred toward Baraduk.

==Biography==
Galasett, a [[Kerestian/Legends|Kerestian]] [[Gender/Legends|male]], was an old friend of [[Reglis Taal]]. Taal's brother, [[Philo]], was brutally murdered by two [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunters]] who believed him to be a wanted criminal; if they had checked their sources, they would have known that there was no [[Bounty/Legends|bounty]] on Philo's head. Nineteen-year-old Taal tracked and killed the bounty hunters himself.<ref name="G71">''[[Galaxy Guide 10: Bounty Hunters]]'', p71</ref> Galasett, knowing of his friend's story, decided that it was unfair and that someone should deal with overzealous bounty hunters; those who exceeded in their job to the point of breaking the law.<ref name="G68">''[[Galaxy Guide 10: Bounty Hunters]]'', p68-69</ref>

Galasett decided to do so himself, becoming a bounty hunter to track other bounty hunters. Although the [[Bounty Hunters' Creed]] prohibited this situation, it did not apply if there was a bounty on the head of the prey. Galasett then started tracking outlaw bounty hunters and capturing them. He became a vigilante for his colleagues. During his career, he was fined for damaging the environment and was also sent to jail for a short time, for hunting in an [[No Hunt Zone|area where it was forbidden to do so]].<ref name="G69" />

At one point, Galasett discovered that the [[Barabel/Legends|Barabel]] bounty hunter, [[Baraduk]], was technically a fugitive [[Slavery/Legends|slave]]—albeit pardoned—and that Baraduk sometimes worked as a [[Bodyguard/Legends|bodyguard]] for figures of organized crime. Labeling Baraduk as an outlaw hunter, Galasett fought the Barabel while he was working for a criminal. During this first encounter, Baraduk, an expert in night attacks due to his natural [[Infrared/Legends|infrared]] vision, took his chance to attack Galasett from darkness. They both were severely wounded in the encounter, but Baraduk managed to leave the planet.<ref name="G67">''[[Galaxy Guide 10: Bounty Hunters]]'', p67</ref>

==Personality and traits==
Galasett despised bounty hunters that had broken the law. He tracked them eagerly and sometimes causing great collateral damage. Whenever he found proof of a bounty hunter committing a crime, Galasett quickly acted so that a bounty on the hunter's head could be posted, and then the Kerestian tried to capture his victim before the other hunter even knew someone wanted him.<ref name="G69" /> After his first encounter with Baraduk, Galasett developed a hatred for the Barabel—and vice versa. Both hunters expected to meet again at a later point and fight until one of them was dead.<ref name="G67" />

==Equipment==
Galasett carried several weapons, including a [[Heavy blaster pistol/Legends|heavy blaster pistol]], a [[Blaster rifle/Legends|blaster rifle]], a [[neurostaff]], a [[Knife/Legends|knife]] and several [[Grenade/Legends|grenades]], including a [[Thermal detonator/Legends|thermal detonator]]. His less offensive gear included a [[Comlink/Legends|comlink]], a [[Datapad/Legends|datapad]], a [[Glowrod/Legends|glowrod]], a [[Medpac/Legends|medpac]] and [[Syntherope/Legends|syntherope]]. He also wore a suit of customized armor.

==Behind the scenes==
Galasett was mentioned in several sections of the [[Roleplaying game|roleplaying]] book ''[[Galaxy Guide 10: Bounty Hunters]]'' ([[1994]]), by [[Rick D. Stuart]], including an image of Galasett by [[Mike Vilardi]]. One of these listed his role-playing stats and a comprehensive list of the gear he carried with him. Most of the other bounty hunters in the book are specified to have an [[Imperial Peace-Keeping Certificate/Legends|Imperial Peace Keeping Certificate]] (or "bounty hunter permit"), except for Galasett, Baraduk, Reglis Taal and [[Dengar/Legends|Dengar]].<ref name="G69" />
